currentUse: First Haitian Baptist Church of Lawrence — an active Créole-language Baptist congregation occupying the former Lithuanian National Catholic church building at 150 Garden Street, Lawrence, MA
summary: The former Sacred Heart of Jesus (Saldžiausios Jėzaus Širdies) Lithuanian National Catholic church building on Garden Street in Lawrence still stands and is in active religious use: it was sold to a Haitian Baptist congregation and today houses the First Haitian Baptist Church of Lawrence, a Créole-speaking congregation. The brick building predates the parish — it was originally the Garden Street Methodist Episcopal Church, acquired by the Lithuanian National Catholic parish in 1917 after the parish's 1916 founding by Stasys B. Mickevičius. Global True Lithuania reports the building was sold in 2001 to the "Haiti Baptists," while Wikipedia and the LNCC's own historical site (lktb.org) state the parish functioned until 2002; the stone plaque reading "Lithuanian National Catholic Church" reportedly still remains near the top of the facade, sun-bleached and barely legible. The community's most durable legacy is the Lithuanian National Catholic Cemetery in neighboring Methuen, founded in 1917, which remains maintained and active: it received a decorative entrance arch around 1997 and a bilingual memorial to Lithuanian-American veterans in 2016, holds roughly 100 graves of Lithuanian US-forces veterans, and is supported by funds from the church sale — matching the archive record that sale proceeds went to the community's cemetery fund. No diocesan involvement exists or existed (the parish was community-owned and independent of Rome), and no recent press, preservation campaigns, or reunion activity was found.

developments.headline: Church building sold to Haitian Baptist congregation
developments.detail: Global True Lithuania reports the former Lithuanian National Catholic church on Garden Street was sold in 2001 to the 'Haiti Baptists'; Wikipedia (citing Vaitkevičienė 2015) and the LNCC historical site lktb.org state the parish functioned until 2002. The sale was carried out by the community itself, and cemetery upkeep is supported by funds from the church sale. No sale price was published in any source found.

sources.title: About the Lithuanian National Catholic Church

developments.headline: Veterans memorial added at the community's Methuen cemetery
developments.detail: The Lithuanian National Catholic Cemetery in Methuen (founded 1917 by the Lawrence parishioners) received a memorial to Lithuanians who fought for the USA in 2016, with Lithuanian and English inscriptions; about 100 Lithuanian US-forces veterans are buried there. The cemetery had earlier received an entrance arch around 1997, retains an outdoor LNCC altar formerly used for Masses and picnics, accepts Ukrainian burials, and is supported by proceeds from the church sale.

developments.headline: Building documented still standing as local-history subject
developments.detail: The Queen City Massachusetts local-history blog documented the building (originally the 1870s-era Garden Street Methodist Episcopal Church, later the Lithuanian National Catholic Church, now the Haitian Baptist church) as still standing in Lawrence.

conflictsWithArchiveRecord: No contradictions on the essentials — the archive's core narrative (community-owned LNCC parish, founded 1916, closed and sold by the community with proceeds to the cemetery fund) is independently confirmed by Global True Lithuania (cemetery "supported through funds from the church sale") and Wikipedia. One date refinement: the archive says closed and sold 2002, and Wikipedia/lktb.org agree the parish functioned until 2002, but Global True Lithuania states the building was sold in 2001 — the sale may have preceded the formal end of parish life by a few months, or one source is off by a year. One unverified detail: the archive's alternate name "Šv. Marijos Tautinė Katalikų Parapija" did not appear in any source found — all sources call the Lawrence parish Sacred Heart of Jesus (Saldžiausios Jėzaus Širdies); this alternate name could not be confirmed or refuted. Minor source-vs-source discrepancy (not vs archive): True Lithuania dates the building to 1855, while Queen City Massachusetts says the Garden Street Methodist Episcopal Church was built in the 1870s.
gaps: Sale price and exact buyer entity for the 2001/2002 transaction were not published in any accessible source; Lawrence/Essex County deed records were not searched. Directory pages for the current occupant (JoinMyChurch, FaithStreet, UnitedStatesChurches) and the Find a Grave / PeopleLegacy cemetery pages all returned HTTP 403 to direct fetch — their contents (address 150 Garden Street matching True Lithuania's "Garden Street (150)", Créole-language ABC-USA congregation, Sunday services 9 AM-12 PM, 342 cemetery memorials at 143 N Lowell St, Methuen) were confirmed only through search-engine excerpts, so current service schedule and 2024-2026 congregational activity are not independently verified. No press coverage of the parish, building, or cemetery from 2020-2026 was found (searched Eagle-Tribune-adjacent terms, church name variants, and Haitian congregation names). Whether the facade plaque still survives today (last documented in True Lithuania's field visit, 2010s) is unverified. The condition and current governing body of the Methuen cemetery fund (who administers it now) was not findable online.
